page: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.112xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 448 virtual CPUs mapped to 224 physical cores of Intel Xeon 8176M processors running at 2.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 6144.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing approximately 13.71 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, reaching 18894.65 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 810.78 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. It also excels in cryptographic operations, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations, where it processes 297.29 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by its lower Geekbench single-core scores and average single-threaded compression results. Multi-threaded Bzip3 decompression and LLM text generation speeds also rank in the lower tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a highly specialized hardware class designed for massive resource density. The extreme memory capacity and high vCPU count eliminate the need for distributed clustering for large datasets, though the lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage. This server is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and intensive data-processing workloads that require massive memory footprints without GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.112xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 448 vCPUs, 224 physical Intel Xeon 8176M cores, and 6144.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 platform delivers a baseline network speed of 100 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high Redis throughput, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are relatively weak. Qualitatively, its massive memory density makes it highly efficient for consolidating large workloads that would otherwise require complex clustering. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise ERP systems, and memory-intensive data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.112xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 448 vCPUs, 6 TB of DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring six terabytes of RAM and massive multi-core processing power for enterprise in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 448 vCPUs and 6144.0 GB of DDR4 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise ERP systems
